lede: A look at January 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including JJ Peterka and Shawn Sawyer, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Sikhio train disaster
events.desc: Thirty-two people are killed when a crane falls onto a passenger train in Sikhio district, Thailand.

events.title: Elvis Presley
events.desc: Elvis Presley's concert Aloha from Hawaii is broadcast live via satellite, and sets the record as the most watched broadcast by an individual entertainer in television history.

events.title: Margrethe II
events.desc: Queen Margrethe II of Denmark ascends the throne, the first Queen of Denmark since 1412 and the first Danish monarch not named Frederik or Christian since 1513.

events.title: USS Enterprise fire
events.desc: USS Enterprise fire: An accidental explosion aboard the USS Enterprise near Hawaii kills 28 people.

events.title: NBC
events.desc: NBC's long-running morning news program Today debuts, with host Dave Garroway.

events.title: Queen Maud Land
events.desc: Norway claims Queen Maud Land in Antarctica.

events.title: 1907 Kingston earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake in Kingston, Jamaica kills more than 1,000 people.

events.title: Giacomo Puccini
events.desc: Giacomo Puccini's Tosca opens in Rome.

events.title: Napoleon III
events.desc: Napoleon III of France escapes an assassination attempt made by Felice Orsini and his accomplices in Paris.

events.title: Andrew III of Hungary
events.desc: Andrew III of Hungary dies, ending the Árpád dynasty in Hungary.

births.name: Dion Forster
births.line: South African minister, theologian, and author

births.name: Mildred Albert
births.line: American fashion commentator, TV and radio personality, and fashion show producer (died 1991)

deaths.text: Robert Ardrey, American-South African author, playwright, and screenwriter
Laura E. Richards, American author and poet
George Dance the Younger, English architect and surveyor
